chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 6: Project 2 — Multi-Step Workflow (Workflow Pattern)
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the sixth chapter of the IVD book. Second hands-on
# project in Part II: build a data processing pipeline (ETL) using IVD,
# introduce workflow-level intents, field mapping, evaluation sections,
# and measure baseline vs IVD context reduction.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_2_practice.chapters[1]
# Source:    recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml, recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml,
#            framework.md, templates/intent.yaml
# Version: 1.1
# Created: 2026-02-09
# Status: Pending
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Guide the reader through building a data processing pipeline (ETL for analytics) using IVD — introducing workflow-level intents, field mapping, evaluation sections, implementing each step with module-level intents, and measuring baseline (read 10 files) vs IVD (read 1 workflow intent) context reduction."

  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Has built a complete, working multi-step data processing pipeline (5+ steps) using IVD
    2. Understands workflow-level intents: what they are, how they differ from module-level intents (Ch5), and when to use them
    3. Has written a workflow-level intent artifact documenting steps, data flow, and error handling
    4. Can apply the workflow-orchestration recipe to orchestrate multi-step processes
    5. Understands optional field mapping (data-field-mapping.yaml) for data transformations between steps
    6. Understands the evaluation section (experimental): quality metrics, improvement loop, bounded iteration
    7. Has implemented each pipeline step with its own module-level intent, linked to the workflow intent
    8. Has measurable evidence: baseline (no IVD, read 10 files) vs IVD (read 1 workflow intent) — 80-90% context reduction
    9. Recognizes the progression: Ch5 module-level → Ch6 workflow-level → next levels in Ch7–10

    This is the second practice project. The reader transitions from single-module intents (Ch5) to orchestrating multiple modules under a workflow-level intent. Code and intent artifacts are the centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale.

  success_metric: |
    - Reader has a working ETL pipeline at the end of the chapter
    - Reader wrote (or followed along with) a workflow-level intent artifact referencing the workflow-orchestration recipe
    - Reader can articulate the difference between module-level (Ch5) and workflow-level intents
    - Reader understands field mapping as an optional section for data transformations
    - Reader understa

nd practice project. The reader transitions from single-module intents (Ch5) to orchestrating multiple modules under a workflow-level intent. Code and intent artifacts are the centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale.

  success_metric: |
    - Reader has a working ETL pipeline at the end of the chapter
    - Reader wrote (or followed along with) a workflow-level intent artifact referencing the workflow-orchestration recipe
    - Reader can articulate the difference between module-level (Ch5) and workflow-level intents
    - Reader understands field mapping as an optional section for data transformations
    - Reader understands the evaluation section as an experimental mechanism for quality measurement and bounded improvement
    - Baseline vs IVD measurement is clear: 80-90% context reduction (read 1 workflow intent vs read 10 files)
    - Reader sees the progression: single agent (Ch5) → orchestrated workflow (Ch6) → infrastructure (Ch7)
    - Chapter ends with pattern recognition bridge to Project 3 (Chapter 7)

  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_2 purpose: build real projects with IVD)"
    - "recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml (canonical recipe)"
    - "recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml (field mapping recipe)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch6)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "6.1"
      title: "Project Overview: ETL Pipeline for Analytics"
      required_content:
        - "What we're building: a data processing pipeline that extracts, transforms, and loads analytics data through 5+ orchestrated steps"
        - "Why this project: multi-step workflows are fundamentally different from single agents — coordination, data flow between steps, error handling, and partial failure require a different intent level"
        - "What the reader will learn: workflow-level intents (NEW), field mapping (optional), evaluation section (experimental), module-level intents per step, baseline vs IVD measurement"
        - "Prerequisites: Part I concepts, Chapter 5 completed (module-level intent practice), basic Python, familiarity with data processing concepts"
        - "End state: working ETL pipeline with workflow-level intent, module-level intents per step, and 80-90% context reduction measured"
        - "Progression context: Ch5 gave you module-level (one agent); this chapter gives you workflow-level (orchestrating multiple modules)"

    - id: "6.2"
      title: "The Problem: Understanding Multi-File Workflows"
      required_content:
        - "The challenge: a multi-step workflow spreads logic across 5-10+ files — an AI agent (or new team member) must read ALL files to understand the system"
        - "Baseline scenario: without IVD, understanding this ETL pipeline requires reading every module, tracing data flow manually, inferring error handling from code, and guessing at the overall orchestration logic"
        - "The cost: reading 10 files = high context window usage, slow onboarding,

this chapter gives you workflow-level (orchestrating multiple modules)"

    - id: "6.2"
      title: "The Problem: Understanding Multi-File Workflows"
      required_content:
        - "The challenge: a multi-step workflow spreads logic across 5-10+ files — an AI agent (or new team member) must read ALL files to understand the system"
        - "Baseline scenario: without IVD, understanding this ETL pipeline requires reading every module, tracing data flow manually, inferring error handling from code, and guessing at the overall orchestration logic"
        - "The cost: reading 10 files = high context window usage, slow onboarding, fragile understanding that breaks when any file changes"
        - "The IVD solution: one workflow-level intent artifact captures the entire pipeline — steps, data flow, error handling, success criteria — readable in minutes"
        - "Key insight: workflow-level intents don't replace module-level intents; they sit ABOVE them, providing the orchestration map that connects individual modules"
        - "Bridge from Ch5: in Ch5 you had one agent with one intent; here you have N modules coordinated by one workflow intent"

    - id: "6.3"
      title: "Step 1: Create Workflow-Level Intent"
      required_content:
        - "Introduce workflow-level intents: scope.level = 'workflow' — a distinct level above module-level (Ch5); workflow intents capture the orchestration of multiple modules into a coordinated process"
        - "The workflow-orchestration recipe: reference recipe, show how it provides the structure for multi-step processes (steps array, data_flow, error_handling, orchestration_pattern)"
        - "Write the workflow-level intent artifact: scope, intent (summary, goal, success_metric), steps array with name/description/input/output/depends_on for each pipeline step"
        - "Document data flow: how data moves between steps (output of step N = input of step N+1), data contracts between modules"
        - "Document error handling: what happens when a step fails (retry, skip, abort pipeline), partial failure strategies"
        - "Show the complete workflow-level YAML artifact inline (the chapter's primary teaching artifact)"
        - "Emphasize: workflow intent is the single document an AI agent reads to understand the entire pipeline — this is the context reduction mechanism"

    - id: "6.4"
      title: "Step 2: Document Steps, Data Flow, Error Handling"
      required_content:
        - "Deep dive into each pipeline step: extract, validate, transform, enrich, load (5+ steps minimum)"
        - "For each step: name, description, input schema, output schema, dependencies, constraints, test path"
        - "Data flow documentation: explicit contracts between steps — what format, what fields, what guarantees"
        - "Error handling patterns: retry with backoff, dead letter queue, circuit breaker, graceful degradation — each with when_to_use and consequence_if_missing"
        - "Orchestration pattern: sequential, parallel where independent, dependency graph"
        - "Emphasize: documenting this in the workflow intent means any AI agent can implement or modify any step without reading the entire co

eps minimum)"
        - "For each step: name, description, input schema, output schema, dependencies, constraints, test path"
        - "Data flow documentation: explicit contracts between steps — what format, what fields, what guarantees"
        - "Error handling patterns: retry with backoff, dead letter queue, circuit breaker, graceful degradation — each with when_to_use and consequence_if_missing"
        - "Orchestration pattern: sequential, parallel where independent, dependency graph"
        - "Emphasize: documenting this in the workflow intent means any AI agent can implement or modify any step without reading the entire codebase"

    - id: "6.5"
      title: "Step 3: Optional - Field Mapping for Data Transformations (recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml)"
      required_content:
        - "Introduce field mapping as an optional IVD section: when data transformations between steps are complex, document them explicitly"
        - "Reference the data-field-mapping recipe: show the structure (source_field → target_field, transformation_rule, validation)"
        - "When to use field mapping: complex ETL transformations, schema mismatches between systems, data normalization across sources"
        - "When NOT to use field mapping: simple pass-through, trivial renames, transforms obvious from code"
        - "Show a field mapping YAML artifact for one of the pipeline's transformation steps"
        - "Emphasize: field mapping is optional — use it when the transformation logic is the hard part, not when the code is self-explanatory"

    - id: "6.6"
      title: "Step 4: Optional - Evaluation Section (quality metrics, improvement loop, bounded iteration)"
      required_content:
        - "Introduce the evaluation section as an experimental IVD mechanism: how the workflow measures its own quality and improves over iterations"
        - "Structure: metrics (what to measure), thresholds (acceptable ranges), improvement_loop (how to iterate), bounded_iteration (when to stop improving)"
        - "Metrics for the ETL pipeline: data completeness, transformation accuracy, processing latency, error rate"
        - "Bounded iteration: define a maximum number of improvement cycles to prevent infinite optimization loops — the workflow knows when 'good enough' is reached"
        - "Why experimental: evaluation is powerful but adds complexity; not every workflow needs it; use when quality measurement is critical to the workflow's purpose"
        - "Show the evaluation section YAML inline, integrated into the workflow intent"

    - id: "6.7"
      title: "Step 5: Implement Each Step (Module-Level Intents)"
      required_content:
        - "Each pipeline step gets its own module-level intent: parent_intent points to the workflow intent, scope.type = 'module'"
        - "Implementation flow: workflow intent → AI reads it → AI implements each step against its module-level intent → each step satisfies both its own constraints and the workflow's data contracts"
        - "Show implementation for 2-3 key steps: extract (API/file ingestion), transform (business logic), load (destination write)"
        - "Code snippets: module structure per step (handler, val

- id: "6.7"
      title: "Step 5: Implement Each Step (Module-Level Intents)"
      required_content:
        - "Each pipeline step gets its own module-level intent: parent_intent points to the workflow intent, scope.type = 'module'"
        - "Implementation flow: workflow intent → AI reads it → AI implements each step against its module-level intent → each step satisfies both its own constraints and the workflow's data contracts"
        - "Show implementation for 2-3 key steps: extract (API/file ingestion), transform (business logic), load (destination write)"
        - "Code snippets: module structure per step (handler, validator, models), following the workflow-orchestration recipe's implementation_pattern"
        - "Emphasize: each module-level intent references its parent workflow intent — bidirectional traceability (Principle 3)"
        - "What if a step's implementation reveals a workflow design issue? Update the workflow intent first, then re-implement — intent is always source of truth"

    - id: "6.8"
      title: "Step 6: End-to-End Verification + Evaluation Loop"
      required_content:
        - "Run the full pipeline end-to-end: all steps execute in sequence, data flows correctly, error handling triggers as expected"
        - "Verification against workflow intent: does the pipeline satisfy all constraints? Do data contracts hold between steps?"
        - "Verification against module intents: does each step satisfy its own constraints?"
        - "Evaluation loop (if evaluation section present): run metrics, compare against thresholds, iterate if below threshold, stop when bounded iteration limit reached or thresholds met"
        - "Show test output: end-to-end pipeline run with metrics (completeness: 98%, latency: 3.2s, error rate: 0.5%)"
        - "Continuous verification: how to keep the pipeline's intent-to-code alignment as the system evolves"

    - id: "6.9"
      title: "Measurement: AI Agent Context Needed (Baseline vs IVD)"
      required_content:
        - "Baseline scenario: build/understand the same pipeline without IVD — AI agent must read all 10+ files, trace data flow through code, infer error handling, piece together orchestration logic"
        - "IVD scenario: AI agent reads 1 workflow-level intent — understands the entire pipeline (steps, data flow, error handling, success criteria) in minutes"
        - "Context reduction measurement: baseline requires ~10 file reads; IVD requires ~1 workflow intent read — 80-90% reduction in context needed"
        - "Why this matters: less context = faster onboarding, fewer hallucinations, less drift, cheaper token usage"
        - "Note: measurements are illustrative from author experience, not controlled experiments (constraint: no_unsourced_statistics)"
        - "Tie back to Chapter 2 (IVD paradigm) and Chapter 5 (first measurement): this is the second data point — the pattern holds across different project types"

    - id: "6.10"
      title: "Results: 80-90% Context Reduction Validated"
      required_content:
        - "Summary of results: workflow-level intents deliver 80-90% context reduction for multi-step systems"
        - "Comparison table: baselin

less context = faster onboarding, fewer hallucinations, less drift, cheaper token usage"
        - "Note: measurements are illustrative from author experience, not controlled experiments (constraint: no_unsourced_statistics)"
        - "Tie back to Chapter 2 (IVD paradigm) and Chapter 5 (first measurement): this is the second data point — the pattern holds across different project types"

    - id: "6.10"
      title: "Results: 80-90% Context Reduction Validated"
      required_content:
        - "Summary of results: workflow-level intents deliver 80-90% context reduction for multi-step systems"
        - "Comparison table: baseline metrics vs IVD metrics (files read, time to understand, iterations to modify, error rate when changing steps)"
        - "Key Points / Summary block (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
        - "Pattern recognition: workflow-level intent sits above module-level intents; together they create a hierarchy that scales"
        - "Bridge to Chapter 7: 'In the next project, you'll tackle infrastructure — background job systems where the challenge is reliability, observability, and long-running processes'"
        - "Checklist: 'Before you move on' — did you write your workflow intent? Did your module intents reference it? Did you measure context reduction? Did your evaluation loop converge?"

  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch6 key_points: multi-step pipeline, workflow-level intent, context reduction 80-90%, evaluation section"
      - "At least one complete workflow-level YAML intent artifact inline (section 6.3)"
      - "At least one field mapping YAML artifact inline (section 6.5)"
      - "At least one evaluation section YAML inline (section 6.6)"
      - "Code snippets for pipeline step implementation (section 6.7) — recipe's implementation_pattern adapted"
      - "Baseline vs IVD comparison table or clear structured comparison (section 6.9–6.10)"
      - "1–2 quotable lines where natural (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Checklist in 6.10 (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Clear bridge to Chapter 7 (infrastructure pattern)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch6; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "Build a data processing pipeline with 5+ steps — the second complete practice project, introducing workflow-level orchestration"
  - "Workflow-level intent (NEW in IVD v1.2): a single artifact that captures the entire pipeline — steps, data flow, error handling, orchestration — so an AI agent reads 1 file instead of 10"
  - "Context reduction: baseline (read 10 files) vs IVD (read 1 workflow intent) — 80-90% reduction validated"
  - "Introduce the evaluation section (experimental): formalize how the workflow measures quality and improves over iterations with bounded iteration to prevent infinite loops"
  - "Field mapping (optional): document complex data transformations between

— the second complete practice project, introducing workflow-level orchestration"
  - "Workflow-level intent (NEW in IVD v1.2): a single artifact that captures the entire pipeline — steps, data flow, error handling, orchestration — so an AI agent reads 1 file instead of 10"
  - "Context reduction: baseline (read 10 files) vs IVD (read 1 workflow intent) — 80-90% reduction validated"
  - "Introduce the evaluation section (experimental): formalize how the workflow measures quality and improves over iterations with bounded iteration to prevent infinite loops"
  - "Field mapping (optional): document complex data transformations between steps using recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml"
  - "Each pipeline step gets its own module-level intent with parent_intent pointing to the workflow intent — bidirectional traceability across levels"
  - "Progression from Ch5: module-level (one agent) → workflow-level (orchestrating multiple modules) → the IVD hierarchy scales"
  - "Pattern recognition: recipe → workflow intent → module intents → implement → verify → measure is repeatable for any multi-step system"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block and a pre-move-on checklist"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section plus checklist"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; grep 'checklist\\|Before you move on' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"

  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
    test: "At least one bold standalone sentence in chapter.md works as a tweet/pull-quote"

  - name: "writing_style_part_2"
    requirement: "Code and intent artifacts are centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale. 'Build this' drives learning."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.code_and_prose.part_2"
    validation: "Manual: code/YAML leads, prose supports; reader is building, not just reading"
    test: "chapter.md contains at least 4 fenced code/YAML blocks; code blocks appear before or alongside explanatory prose, not after long prose-only passages"

  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms bolded on first use; code font for file paths and commands; no paragraph exceeds 10 sentences without subheading"

  - name: "recipe_alignment"
    requirement: "Workflow implementation must align with recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml structure (steps, d

er long prose-only passages"

  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms bolded on first use; code font for file paths and commands; no paragraph exceeds 10 sentences without subheading"

  - name: "recipe_alignment"
    requirement: "Workflow implementation must align with recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml structure (steps, data_flow, error_handling, orchestration_pattern) AND recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml for field mapping sections"
    source: "recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml, recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml"
    validation: "Chapter's YAML artifacts and code snippets match recipe patterns; no contradictions with either recipe"
    test: "Steps structure, data flow, error handling, and field mapping in chapter.md match recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml and recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml; diff produces zero contradictions"

  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch6 aligns with framework principles and recipes; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle, optional section, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"

  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless in examples/code"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding code/examples) returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' chapter.md outside fenced code blocks returns 0 matches"

  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "Baseline vs IVD measurements must be marked as illustrative (author experience) unless backed by controlled study"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    validation: "Measurement section includes caveat about illustrative data"
    test: "Section 6.9 contains explicit caveat text (e.g., 'illustrative', 'author experience', 'not a controlled study')"

  - name: "author_sourced_examples"
    requirement: "The ETL pipeline scenario and code examples should reflect the author's real experience with the workflow-orchestration recipe and data processing patterns"
    source: "recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml.examples"
    validation: "Manual: examples feel real, not generic; reference actual recipe patterns"
    test: "ETL scenario references recipe patterns (steps, data_flow, error_handling); no generic 'widget factory' or 'todo app' examples"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 6 i

cenario and code examples should reflect the author's real experience with the workflow-orchestration recipe and data processing patterns"
    source: "recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml.examples"
    validation: "Manual: examples feel real, not generic; reference actual recipe patterns"
    test: "ETL scenario references recipe patterns (steps, data_flow, error_handling); no generic 'widget factory' or 'todo app' examples"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 6 is the second practice project — ETL pipeline using the workflow-orchestration recipe, introducing workflow-level intents. Part II sequence: Ch5 agent (beginner), Ch6 workflow (intermediate), Ch7 infrastructure, Ch8 integration, Ch9 full-stack, Ch10 existing codebase."

  reasoning: |
    - Part II purpose: build real projects with IVD; learn by doing. Ch6 is the second project — intermediate complexity, introduces a new intent level.
    - ETL pipeline chosen because: (1) multi-step workflows are ubiquitous in software engineering, (2) data flow between steps makes the orchestration challenge tangible, (3) context reduction is dramatic (10 files → 1 workflow intent), (4) field mapping and evaluation sections have natural use cases in data processing.
    - Workflow-level intents are NEW in IVD v1.2: this chapter is the first place the reader encounters them. Ch5 covered module-level; Ch6 shows the next level in the hierarchy. The reader sees that IVD scales — individual modules compose into workflows.
    - Field mapping (optional) is introduced here because data transformations are the natural use case — mapping fields between systems, normalizing schemas, documenting transformation logic that's hard to infer from code alone.
    - Evaluation section (experimental) is introduced here because ETL pipelines have natural quality metrics (completeness, accuracy, latency) — the reader sees how a workflow can measure and improve itself.
    - Context reduction (80-90%) is the chapter's measurable outcome, building on Ch5's measurement. Part III (Evidence) will aggregate these per-project measurements.

  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_2, Ch6 key_points, placeholder); recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml (validated recipe); recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml (field mapping recipe); framework.md (IVD flow, intent levels)"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Use a microservices deployment workflow instead of ETL pipeline"
    rejected_because: "Microservices deployment conflates workflow orchestration with infrastructure concerns (Ch7 territory). ETL keeps the focus on data flow and step coordination, making workflow-level intents the clear protagonist."
    when_it_works: "If the audience is exclusively DevOps engineers who relate more to deployment pipelines than data processing."

  - name:

=========================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Use a microservices deployment workflow instead of ETL pipeline"
    rejected_because: "Microservices deployment conflates workflow orchestration with infrastructure concerns (Ch7 territory). ETL keeps the focus on data flow and step coordination, making workflow-level intents the clear protagonist."
    when_it_works: "If the audience is exclusively DevOps engineers who relate more to deployment pipelines than data processing."

  - name: "Introduce workflow-level intents in Part I (theory) rather than Part II (practice)"
    rejected_because: "Part I already introduces the four levels conceptually. Showing workflow-level intents in practice — with a real project — is more powerful than explaining them in theory. The reader needs to have written a module-level intent (Ch5) before the workflow level makes sense."
    when_it_works: "If the book had a longer Part I with room for extended examples at each level."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Reader skipped Chapter 5 and doesn't understand module-level intents"
    action: "Include a brief recap of module-level intents in 6.1 (prerequisites); reference Ch5 explicitly; make the progression clear without requiring re-reading Ch5"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'I was lost because I didn't do Ch5'"
    severity: "medium"

  - condition: "Workflow-level intent feels like unnecessary overhead for a 5-step pipeline"
    action: "Show the baseline cost first (6.2): reading 10 files, tracing data flow through code, inferring orchestration. Make the overhead/payoff tradeoff visceral. Small pipeline, but the pattern scales."
    monitor: "Reviewer feedback: 'Why do I need a workflow intent for something this simple?'"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "Field mapping and evaluation sections overwhelm the chapter — too many new concepts"
    action: "Both are explicitly optional in the chapter flow (sections 6.5 and 6.6). Reader can skip them on first pass and return later. Mark them clearly as 'Optional' in section titles."
    monitor: "Page count; beta reader feedback on cognitive load"
    severity: "medium"

  - condition: "Chapter is too long — more sections (10) than Ch5 (7)"
    action: "Target 30–40 pages; keep code focused on key snippets (not full listing); sections 6.5 and 6.6 are shorter (optional concepts, not full implementations); reference recipe and repo for complete code"
    monitor: "Page count; beta reader completion rate for this chapter"
    severity: "medium"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/part-2-practice/chapter-06-multi-step-workflow/chapter_06_multi_step_workflow_intent.yaml"
  output: "manuscript/part-2-practice/chapter-06

eep code focused on key snippets (not full listing); sections 6.5 and 6.6 are shorter (optional concepts, not full implementations); reference recipe and repo for complete code"
    monitor: "Page count; beta reader completion rate for this chapter"
    severity: "medium"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/part-2-practice/chapter-06-multi-step-workflow/chapter_06_multi_step_workflow_intent.yaml"
  output: "manuscript/part-2-practice/chapter-06-multi-step-workflow/chapter.md"
  status: "pending"
  version: 1

  process:
    reference: "book/chapter_writing_process_intent.yaml"
    current_step: "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    completed_steps:
      - "step_1_verify_prerequisites"
      - "step_2_create_chapter_folder"
      - "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    pending_steps:
      - "step_4_write_manuscript"
      - "step_5_cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent, recipes, and IVD framework)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent Ch6 appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (6.1–6.10) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary block and pre-move-on checklist"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    - check: "At least one complete workflow-level YAML intent artifact inline (section 6.3)"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[6.3]"
    - check: "At least one field mapping YAML artifact inline (section 6.5)"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[6.5]"
    - check: "At least one evaluation section YAML inline (section 6.6)"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[6.6]"
    - check: "Code snippets match recipe implementation_pattern (handler, validator, models per step)"
      validates: "constraints.recipe_alignment"
    - check: "Workflow intent steps match recipe workflow-orchestration.yaml structure (steps, data_flow, error_handling)"
      validates: "constraints.recipe_alignment"
    - check: "Baseline vs IVD comparison present with 80-90% context reduction measurement and caveat about illustrative data"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[6.9], constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    - check: "Writing style: code/YAML centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale (Part II style)"
      validates: "constraints.writing_style_part_2"
    - check: "Bridge to Chapter 7 (infrastructure pattern) present in 6.10"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[6.10]"
    - check: "No contradictions with ivd_system_intent.yaml, book_system_intent.yaml, recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml, or recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml"
      validates: "constraints.master_intent_alignment, constraints.recipe_

surement and caveat about illustrative data"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[6.9], constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    - check: "Writing style: code/YAML centerpiece; prose explains steps and rationale (Part II style)"
      validates: "constraints.writing_style_part_2"
    - check: "Bridge to Chapter 7 (infrastructure pattern) present in 6.10"
      validates: "deliverables.sections[6.10]"
    - check: "No contradictions with ivd_system_intent.yaml, book_system_intent.yaml, recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml, or recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml"
      validates: "constraints.master_intent_alignment, constraints.recipe_alignment"

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.1"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: "Fixed section 6.3 required_content: workflow-level intents use scope.level = 'workflow', not 'module'. The four IVD levels are system, workflow, module, task — workflow is a distinct level above module."
    reason: "Canon accuracy: scope.level must match IVD's four-level hierarchy as defined in templates/intent_levels_guide.md and framework.md."
  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Initial chapter intent for Chapter 6 'Project 2: Multi-Step Workflow (Workflow Pattern)'.
      Derived from book_system_intent.yaml Ch6 (key_points, placeholder), recipes/workflow-orchestration.yaml
      (steps, data_flow, error_handling, orchestration_pattern), recipes/data-field-mapping.yaml
      (field mapping structure), framework.md (IVD flow, intent levels), and templates/intent.yaml
      (canonical structure).
      Sections 6.1–6.10 with required_content, constraints, rationale, alternatives, risks, verification.
      Part II writing style applied (code centerpiece, prose supports).
      Introduces workflow-level intents (NEW in IVD v1.2), field mapping (optional), and evaluation
      section (experimental).
      Recipe-specific constraints: recipe_alignment (workflow-orchestration.yaml AND data-field-mapping.yaml),
      author_sourced_examples.
      Risks identified: skipped Ch5, overhead perception, cognitive load, chapter length.
    reason: "IVD: one intent per chapter; Ch6 is the second practice project introducing workflow-level intents. Intent-only; manuscript pending."
